title: Implement CircuitBreakerManager singleton for per-host circuit breakers
description: Create the CircuitBreakerManager singleton that manages one CircuitBreaker instance per host. Uses ConcurrentDictionary<string, CircuitBreaker> for thread-safe per-host breaker management. Provides GetCircuitBreaker(host) method that returns existing or creates new circuit breaker. Include comprehensive unit tests following existing xUnit patterns (no mocking framework, manual test doubles).
design_context: Section 3.3 of telemetry-design.md: CircuitBreakerManager is a singleton that manages circuit breakers per host. Uses ConcurrentDictionary for thread safety. GetCircuitBreaker(host) creates or returns existing breaker. Sprint plan Task 2: CircuitBreakerManager depends on CircuitBreaker (already complete).
input_requirements: CircuitBreaker class exists at src/Telemetry/CircuitBreaker.cs (Polly-based, internal sealed class with ExecuteAsync, State, Reset)
CircuitBreakerState enum exists in CircuitBreaker.cs (Closed, Open, HalfOpen)
TelemetryConfiguration exists with CircuitBreakerThreshold and CircuitBreakerTimeout properties
exit_criteria: CircuitBreakerManager is internal sealed class with singleton GetInstance() pattern
GetCircuitBreaker(host) returns same instance for same host
GetCircuitBreaker(host) returns different instances for different hosts
Thread-safe for concurrent access from multiple connections
Unit tests: GetCircuitBreaker_NewHost_CreatesBreaker, GetCircuitBreaker_SameHost_ReturnsSameBreaker, GetCircuitBreaker_DifferentHosts_CreatesSeparateBreakers
All unit tests pass (dotnet test --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~CircuitBreakerManager')
Full solution builds successfully (dotnet build)
test_requirements: GetCircuitBreaker_NewHost_CreatesBreaker: new host returns non-null CircuitBreaker
GetCircuitBreaker_SameHost_ReturnsSameBreaker: same host twice returns same reference
GetCircuitBreaker_DifferentHosts_CreatesSeparateBreakers: 'host1' and 'host2' return different instances
GetCircuitBreaker_ThreadSafe_NoDuplicates: 10 concurrent threads requesting same host get same instance
Follow xUnit [Fact] pattern with Arrange/Act/Assert, same naming convention as CircuitBreakerTests.cs

title: Implement CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter wrapping ITelemetryExporter with circuit breaker
description: Create CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter that implements ITelemetryExporter and wraps an inner exporter with circuit breaker protection. When circuit is closed, delegates to inner exporter. When circuit is open, drops events gracefully (returns false, no exception). Circuit breaker tracks failures from inner exporter. Uses CircuitBreakerManager to get per-host circuit breaker. All exceptions swallowed internally per telemetry design requirement. Include comprehensive unit tests.
design_context: Section 3.3 and Section 8.1 of telemetry-design.md: CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter implements ITelemetryExporter. Wraps inner exporter with circuit breaker. Drops events when circuit open. Catches BrokenCircuitException and returns false. Circuit breaker MUST see exceptions before they are swallowed. Sprint plan Task 3.
input_requirements: CircuitBreaker class with ExecuteAsync, State property
ITelemetryExporter interface with ExportAsync(IReadOnlyList<TelemetryFrontendLog>, CancellationToken) returning Task<bool>
exit_criteria: CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter implements ITelemetryExporter (public interface)
Constructor takes (string host, ITelemetryExporter innerExporter)
Gets circuit breaker from CircuitBreakerManager.GetInstance().GetCircuitBreaker(host)
When circuit closed: delegates to innerExporter.ExportAsync(), returns its result
When circuit open: returns false, no exception thrown to caller
Circuit breaker tracks inner exporter failures (sees exceptions before swallowing)
All exceptions caught internally, logged at TRACE level via Debug.WriteLine
Unit tests pass, solution builds
test_requirements: CircuitClosed_ExportsMetrics: circuit closed, inner exporter called, returns true
CircuitOpen_DropsMetrics: circuit open, no export, returns false, no exception
InnerExporterFails_CircuitBreakerTracksFailure: inner exporter throws, circuit breaker failure count incremented
InnerExporterReturnsTrue_ReturnsTrue: success passthrough
InnerExporterReturnsFalse_ReturnsFalse: failure passthrough
NullLogs_HandledGracefully: null/empty input doesn't crash
Use manual mock implementing ITelemetryExporter (no mocking framework), follow existing test patterns

title: Implement ITelemetryClient interface, TelemetryClient, and TelemetryClientHolder
description: Create ITelemetryClient interface with Enqueue, FlushAsync, CloseAsync methods. Implement TelemetryClient using ConcurrentQueue for batching, periodic Timer flush (default 5s), batch size threshold flush, SemaphoreSlim for flush serialization, graceful shutdown. Create TelemetryClientHolder class for reference counting. All exceptions swallowed internally. Include comprehensive unit tests.
design_context: Section 3.2 of telemetry-design.md: ITelemetryClient is internal interface extending IAsyncDisposable. TelemetryClient uses ConcurrentQueue, Timer for periodic flush, SemaphoreSlim for preventing concurrent flushes. Enqueue is non-blocking thread-safe. FlushAsync drains queue up to batch size. CloseAsync stops timer, cancels pending operations, does final flush. TelemetryClientHolder holds client and int _refCount starting at 1.
input_requirements: ITelemetryExporter interface exists with ExportAsync method
TelemetryConfiguration exists with BatchSize (100) and FlushIntervalMs (5000)
output_deliverables: src/Telemetry/ITelemetryClient.cs - Interface with Enqueue, FlushAsync, CloseAsync
src/Telemetry/TelemetryClient.cs - Full batching implementation with timer
exit_criteria: ITelemetryClient is internal interface extending IAsyncDisposable with void Enqueue(TelemetryFrontendLog), Task FlushAsync(CancellationToken), Task CloseAsync()
TelemetryClient constructor takes (ITelemetryExporter, TelemetryConfiguration)
Enqueue is non-blocking, adds to ConcurrentQueue, triggers flush if batch size reached
FlushAsync uses SemaphoreSlim(1,1) to prevent concurrent flushes, drains queue up to batch size
CloseAsync: sets _disposed, disposes timer, cancels CTS, does final flush, disposes resources
Timer calls FlushAsync periodically at FlushIntervalMs
All exceptions swallowed (caught, Debug.WriteLine at TRACE)
TelemetryClientHolder has internal int _refCount=1 and ITelemetryClient Client property
All unit tests pass, solution builds
test_requirements: Enqueue_AddsToQueue_NonBlocking: enqueue returns immediately, event in queue
Enqueue_BatchSizeReached_TriggersFlush: enqueue 100 events (batch size), ExportAsync called
FlushTimer_Elapses_ExportsEvents: wait for flush interval, ExportAsync called
CloseAsync_FlushesRemainingEvents: events in queue then close, all events exported
Exception_Swallowed_NoThrow: exporter throws, no exception propagated
Enqueue_AfterDispose_NoOp: enqueue after CloseAsync does nothing
FlushAsync_EmptyQueue_NoExport: flush with no events doesn't call exporter
ConcurrentFlush_OnlyOneProceeds: two concurrent FlushAsync calls, only one export
Use manual mock ITelemetryExporter (captures ExportAsync calls)

title: Implement TelemetryClientManager singleton with per-host client management
description: Create TelemetryClientManager singleton that manages one TelemetryClient per host with reference counting. Uses ConcurrentDictionary<string, TelemetryClientHolder>. GetOrCreateClient(host, exporterFactory, config) creates new client or increments ref count. ReleaseClientAsync(host) decrements ref count, closes and removes when zero. Include test injection support (UseTestInstance, Reset). Include comprehensive unit tests.
design_context: Section 3.2 of telemetry-design.md: TelemetryClientManager is singleton. GetOrCreateClient uses AddOrUpdate on ConcurrentDictionary - factory creates new TelemetryClientHolder with new TelemetryClient, update increments _refCount. ReleaseClientAsync decrements _refCount, if 0 removes from dict and calls CloseAsync. Also supports UseTestInstance() for test isolation and Reset() for cleanup. Sprint plan Task 1.
input_requirements: ITelemetryClient interface with Enqueue, FlushAsync, CloseAsync
output_deliverables: src/Telemetry/TelemetryClientManager.cs - Singleton manager with ref counting
exit_criteria: TelemetryClientManager is internal sealed class with static GetInstance()
GetOrCreateClient(host, Func<ITelemetryExporter> exporterFactory, TelemetryConfiguration config) returns ITelemetryClient
Same host returns same client, increments ref count
ReleaseClientAsync(host) decrements ref count, closes+removes client when count reaches 0
Thread-safe for concurrent access
UseTestInstance() for test injection, Reset() for cleanup
All unit tests pass, solution builds
test_requirements: GetOrCreateClient_NewHost_CreatesClient: new host returns non-null client with RefCount=1
GetOrCreateClient_ExistingHost_ReturnsSameClient: same host twice returns same client, RefCount=2
ReleaseClientAsync_LastReference_ClosesClient: single ref then release, client CloseAsync called, removed from cache
ReleaseClientAsync_MultipleReferences_KeepsClient: two refs, release one, RefCount=1, client still active
GetOrCreateClient_ThreadSafe_NoDuplicates: 10 concurrent threads same host, single client created
ReleaseClientAsync_UnknownHost_NoOp: release non-existent host doesn't throw
UseTestInstance_ReplacesAndRestores: test instance replaces singleton, IDisposable restores original
Use manual mock ITelemetryClient and ITelemetryExporter

title: Implement StatementTelemetryContext for multi-activity data aggregation
description: Create StatementTelemetryContext class that holds aggregated telemetry data for a single statement. Implements MergeFrom(Activity) method that merges activity tags/events based on OperationName. Handles connection-level tags (session.id, auth.type, system config, connection params), statement-level tags (statement.id, statement.type, result.format), CloudFetch chunk details (from download_summary events), polling metrics, error info, and result latency. Builds OssSqlDriverTelemetryLog proto message. Include comprehensive unit tests.
design_context: Section 2.3, 3.5, 4.4.2-4.4.12 of telemetry-design.md: StatementTelemetryContext merges data from multiple activities. MergeFrom switches on OperationName: ExecuteQuery/ExecuteUpdate → statement metrics; DownloadFiles → chunk details; PollOperationStatus → polling metrics. Also captures DriverSystemConfiguration (driver.version, runtime.*, os.*), DriverConnectionParameters (connection.*, feature.*), and DriverErrorInfo. BuildProto creates OssSqlDriverTelemetryLog with all nested messages.
input_requirements: Proto generated classes exist (OssSqlDriverTelemetryLog, DriverSystemConfiguration, DriverConnectionParameters, SqlExecutionEvent, ChunkDetails, ResultLatency, OperationDetail, DriverErrorInfo)
TelemetryFrontendLog model exists with FrontendLogEventId and WorkspaceId
output_deliverables: src/Telemetry/StatementTelemetryContext.cs - Data aggregation context with MergeFrom and BuildProto
exit_criteria: StatementTelemetryContext is internal sealed class
Constructor takes optional (string? sessionId, DriverSystemConfiguration? systemConfig, DriverConnectionParameters? connectionParams)
MergeFrom(Activity) merges tags based on OperationName (ExecuteQuery, ExecuteUpdate, DownloadFiles, PollOperationStatus, GetCatalogs, etc.)
Captures session.id, statement.id, auth.type, statement.type, result.format, result.compression_enabled
Processes cloudfetch.download_summary ActivityEvent for chunk details
Captures poll.count, poll.latency_ms for operation detail
Captures error.type and activity status for error info
BuildProto() returns complete OssSqlDriverTelemetryLog proto
All unit tests pass, solution builds
test_requirements: MergeFrom_ExecuteQuery_CapturesLatencyAndType: root activity sets OperationLatencyMs and StatementType
MergeFrom_DownloadFiles_CapturesChunkDetails: CloudFetch event tags extracted to ChunkDetails
MergeFrom_PollStatus_CapturesPollingMetrics: poll.count and poll.latency_ms captured
MergeFrom_MultipleActivities_AggregatesCorrectly: 3 activities for same statement merge into one context
MergeFrom_ErrorActivity_CapturesErrorInfo: error.type and status description captured
MergeFrom_ConnectionActivity_CapturesSystemConfig: driver.version, os.*, runtime.* captured
BuildProto_ReturnsCompleteMessage: all fields populated in OssSqlDriverTelemetryLog
BuildProto_MissingFields_DefaultToEmptyOrZero: missing tags result in empty strings and 0 values
Use real Activity objects created from ActivitySource in tests

title: Implement MetricsAggregator for per-connection activity-to-proto aggregation
description: Create MetricsAggregator class that receives ProcessActivity callbacks, aggregates activities by statement_id into StatementTelemetryContext, detects root activity completion to emit proto via ITelemetryClient.Enqueue(). Uses ConcurrentDictionary<string, StatementTelemetryContext> keyed by statement_id. Handles connection open events (emit immediately), statement events (aggregate until root completes), and error events. Implements FlushAsync for connection close. Filters tags using TelemetryTagRegistry. All exceptions swallowed. Include comprehensive unit tests.
design_context: Section 3.5 of telemetry-design.md: MetricsAggregator is per-connection. ProcessActivity extracts statement.id, gets/creates StatementTelemetryContext, calls MergeFrom. IsRootActivity checks parent == null or parent from different ActivitySource. When root completes, builds TelemetryFrontendLog from proto and calls _telemetryClient.Enqueue(). Connection.Open activities emit immediately. FlushAsync on connection close emits all pending contexts. Sprint plan Task 5.
input_requirements: ExceptionClassifier for terminal vs retryable exceptions
output_deliverables: src/Telemetry/MetricsAggregator.cs - Per-connection aggregator with ProcessActivity and FlushAsync
exit_criteria: MetricsAggregator is internal sealed class implementing IDisposable
Constructor takes (ITelemetryClient, TelemetryConfiguration) plus optional session/system config params
ProcessActivity(Activity) routes to correct StatementTelemetryContext by statement.id
Root activity completion (no parent or parent from different source) triggers proto emission via Enqueue
Connection.Open activities emit immediately without statement_id aggregation
FlushAsync emits all pending statement contexts (for connection close)
IsRootActivity correctly identifies root vs child activities
All exceptions swallowed (try-catch with Debug.WriteLine at TRACE)
All unit tests pass, solution builds
test_requirements: ProcessActivity_Statement_AggregatesByStatementId: multiple activities with same statement_id go to same context
ProcessActivity_RootActivityComplete_EmitsProto: root activity stops, telemetryClient.Enqueue called
ProcessActivity_ChildActivity_DoesNotEmit: child activity stops, no Enqueue until root stops
ProcessActivity_ConnectionOpen_EmitsImmediately: Connection.Open activity triggers immediate emission
ProcessActivity_NoStatementId_Skipped: activity without statement.id is ignored
FlushAsync_EmitsPendingContexts: pending contexts emitted on flush
ProcessActivity_ExceptionSwallowed: aggregator error doesn't propagate
ProcessActivity_MultipleStatements_SeparateContexts: different statement_ids tracked separately
Use manual mock ITelemetryClient, real Activity objects from ActivitySource

title: Implement DatabricksActivityListener as global singleton routing activities to aggregators
description: Create DatabricksActivityListener as a global singleton that wraps .NET ActivityListener to subscribe to 'Databricks.Adbc.Driver' ActivitySource. Routes ActivityStopped callbacks to the appropriate MetricsAggregator based on session.id tag. Supports RegisterAggregator/UnregisterAggregatorAsync for connection lifecycle. Sampling controlled by whether any aggregators are registered. All exceptions swallowed in callbacks. Include comprehensive unit tests.
design_context: Section 3.4 of telemetry-design.md: DatabricksActivityListener is a global singleton (Lazy<T>). Wraps ActivityListener subscribing to 'Databricks.Adbc.Driver'. Routes ActivityStopped to MetricsAggregator by session.id. RegisterAggregator(sessionId, aggregator) on connection open. UnregisterAggregatorAsync(sessionId) on connection close (flushes aggregator). Start() creates and registers the ActivityListener. Sampling returns AllDataAndRecorded when aggregators exist. Sprint plan Task 6.
exit_criteria: DatabricksActivityListener is internal sealed class implementing IDisposable
Singleton via static Lazy<DatabricksActivityListener> or similar pattern
RegisterAggregator(string sessionId, MetricsAggregator aggregator) adds to ConcurrentDictionary
UnregisterAggregatorAsync(string sessionId) removes aggregator, calls FlushAsync on it
Start() creates ActivityListener with ShouldListenTo = 'Databricks.Adbc.Driver'
ActivityStopped callback extracts session.id, routes to correct aggregator
Sample returns AllDataAndRecorded when aggregators registered, None otherwise
All exceptions swallowed in OnActivityStopped (try-catch with Debug.WriteLine)
All unit tests pass, solution builds
test_requirements: Start_ListensToDatabricksActivitySource: ShouldListenTo returns true for 'Databricks.Adbc.Driver'
ActivityStopped_RoutesToCorrectAggregator: activity with session.id routes to registered aggregator
ActivityStopped_NoSessionId_Ignored: activity without session.id tag is silently dropped
ActivityStopped_UnknownSessionId_Ignored: activity with unregistered session.id is silently dropped
ActivityStopped_ExceptionSwallowed: aggregator throws, no exception propagated
RegisterAggregator_AddsToRegistry: registered aggregator receives activities
UnregisterAggregatorAsync_FlushesAndRemoves: unregister flushes aggregator and removes from registry
Sample_NoAggregators_ReturnsNone: no registered aggregators, sampling returns None
Sample_WithAggregators_ReturnsAllData: registered aggregators, sampling returns AllDataAndRecorded

title: Integrate telemetry pipeline into DatabricksConnection lifecycle
description: Modify DatabricksConnection to initialize and manage the telemetry pipeline. In OpenAsync: parse TelemetryConfiguration, check feature flag, create TelemetryClient via TelemetryClientManager, create per-connection MetricsAggregator, register with DatabricksActivityListener. In Dispose: unregister aggregator, flush, release client via TelemetryClientManager. Add TestExporterFactory for test injection. Wire CreateTelemetryExporter to create CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter wrapping DatabricksTelemetryExporter.
design_context: Section 3.2 (Usage in DatabricksConnection), Section 9 (Graceful Shutdown) of telemetry-design.md: OpenAsync checks _telemetryConfig.Enabled && IsTelemetryFeatureFlagEnabled(), then gets shared client from TelemetryClientManager, creates MetricsAggregator, creates DatabricksActivityListener, calls Start(). DisposeAsync stops listener, flushes aggregator, releases client. TestExporterFactory allows test injection. Sprint plan Task 7.
input_requirements: FeatureFlagCache (already complete) for feature flag checks
exit_criteria: DatabricksConnection has private fields: ITelemetryClient?, MetricsAggregator?, _telemetryConfig
OpenAsync: parses TelemetryConfiguration from Properties, checks feature flag, initializes telemetry pipeline
CreateTelemetryExporter: creates DatabricksTelemetryExporter wrapped in CircuitBreakerTelemetryExporter
TestExporterFactory internal property allows test injection
Dispose: unregisters aggregator from listener, flushes, releases client, releases feature flag context
All telemetry exceptions swallowed in connection lifecycle
Telemetry skipped when feature flag disabled
Existing tests still pass, solution builds
test_requirements: OpenAsync_TelemetryEnabled_InitializesPipeline: when enabled, TelemetryClientManager.GetOrCreateClient called
OpenAsync_FeatureFlagDisabled_NoTelemetry: when disabled, no telemetry client created
Dispose_ReleasesTelemetryClient: connection dispose calls ReleaseClientAsync
Dispose_FlushesMetricsBeforeRelease: pending metrics flushed before client release
TestExporterFactory_UsedWhenSet: test factory creates exporter instead of default
Dispose_ExceptionSwallowed: telemetry cleanup error doesn't propagate
Note: These may need to be integration-style tests using real connections or careful mocking

title: Add telemetry tags to connection activities (session.id, system config, connection params)
description: Enhance DatabricksConnection to set telemetry-specific Activity tags during connection open. Add session.id, auth.type, driver.version, driver.name, runtime.*, os.*, client.app_name, locale.name, char_set_encoding, process.name, connection.http_path, connection.host, connection.port, connection.mode, connection.auth_mech, connection.auth_flow, feature.* tags. These tags are set on the Connection.Open activity and are used by MetricsAggregator to populate DriverSystemConfiguration and DriverConnectionParameters in the proto.
design_context: Section 4.4.2-4.4.4 of telemetry-design.md: Connection activities need tags for proto population. SetSystemConfigurationTags sets driver.*, runtime.*, os.*, client.*, locale.*, char_set_encoding, process.name. SetConnectionParameterTags sets connection.*, feature.*, auth.*. Tags set in CreateSessionRequest() and HandleOpenSessionResponse() on the Activity? parameter. Sprint plan Task 8 (connection portion).
input_requirements: DatabricksConnection.cs with existing CreateSessionRequest and HandleOpenSessionResponse methods
output_deliverables: Modified src/DatabricksConnection.cs with SetSystemConfigurationTags and SetConnectionParameterTags methods
Test verification that tags are set on activities
exit_criteria: session.id tag set from Thrift session handle in HandleOpenSessionResponse
auth.type tag set based on authentication method (pat, oauth-m2m, etc.)
Driver tags set: driver.version, driver.name
Runtime tags set: runtime.name, runtime.version, runtime.vendor
OS tags set: os.name, os.version, os.arch
Client tags set: client.app_name, locale.name, char_set_encoding, process.name
Connection tags set: connection.http_path, connection.host, connection.port, connection.mode
Auth tags set: connection.auth_mech, connection.auth_flow
Feature tags set: feature.arrow, feature.direct_results, feature.cloudfetch, feature.lz4
All existing tests still pass, solution builds
test_requirements: Verify Connection.Open activity has session.id tag after open
Verify system config tags (driver.version, os.name, runtime.name) are set
Verify connection parameter tags (connection.host, connection.mode) are set
Verify feature flags tags (feature.cloudfetch, feature.lz4) are set
Verify auth tags (auth.type, connection.auth_mech) are set
Note: may require integration tests with real connection or careful activity capture

title: Add telemetry tags to statement and CloudFetch activities (statement.id, result.format, chunk metrics, poll metrics)
description: Enhance DatabricksStatement and CloudFetch/Reader components to set telemetry-specific Activity tags. Add statement.id, session.id, statement.type tags to all statement/metadata activities. Propagate session.id and statement.id to child activities. Add result.format, result.compression_enabled, poll.count, poll.latency_ms tags. Add cloudfetch.download_summary ActivityEvent with chunk metrics. Add result.ready_latency_ms and result.consumption_latency_ms tags.
design_context: Section 2.3, 4.4.5-4.4.8, 4.4.10 of telemetry-design.md: Statement activities need statement.id and session.id tags. Child activities must explicitly propagate these tags (not inherited in .NET). CloudFetch downloads emit cloudfetch.download_summary event with total_files, successful_downloads, total_time_ms, initial_chunk_latency_ms, slowest_chunk_latency_ms. Polling emits poll.count and poll.latency_ms. Result latency tracked via result.ready_latency_ms. Sprint plan Task 8 (statement/CloudFetch portion).
output_deliverables: Modified src/DatabricksStatement.cs with statement telemetry tags
Modified CloudFetch/Reader components with chunk and result latency tags
exit_criteria: All statement/metadata activities have statement.id and session.id tags
Child activities propagate session.id and statement.id from parent
statement.type tag set (query, update, metadata)
result.format tag set (cloudfetch, inline_arrow)
result.compression_enabled tag set
CloudFetch downloads emit cloudfetch.download_summary ActivityEvent with chunk metrics
poll.count and poll.latency_ms tags set on polling activities
result.ready_latency_ms tag set on statement activities
All existing tests still pass, solution builds
test_requirements: Statement activity has statement.id and session.id tags
Metadata activities (GetCatalogs, etc.) have statement.id and session.id tags
Child activity propagates routing tags from parent
CloudFetch download summary event contains correct chunk metrics
Note: some tags may need integration tests or careful mocking of activity context

title: Create E2E telemetry test infrastructure (CapturingExporter, TelemetryTestHelpers)
description: Create test infrastructure for E2E telemetry testing. Implement CapturingTelemetryExporter that captures exported TelemetryFrontendLog instances. Create TelemetryTestHelpers with CreateConnectionWithTelemetry and CreateConnectionWithCapturingTelemetry helper methods. These helpers set up DatabricksConnection with TestExporterFactory injected for test capture without real HTTP calls to the telemetry endpoint.
design_context: Section 3.2 (Test Injection Pattern) of telemetry-design.md: CapturingTelemetryExporter implements ITelemetryExporter, stores logs in a ConcurrentBag/List. TelemetryTestHelpers.CreateConnectionWithTelemetry creates connection with injected mock exporter. CreateConnectionWithCapturingTelemetry combines both. Test flow: create connection with capturing exporter → execute operations → assert on captured logs. Sprint plan Task 9 test infrastructure.
input_requirements: Environment variables for Databricks connection (DATABRICKS_HOST, DATABRICKS_TOKEN, DATABRICKS_HTTP_PATH)
output_deliverables: test/E2E/Telemetry/TelemetryTestHelpers.cs - Helper methods for telemetry test setup
exit_criteria: CapturingTelemetryExporter implements ITelemetryExporter, thread-safe log capture
TelemetryTestHelpers.CreateConnectionWithTelemetry builds connection with injected exporter
TelemetryTestHelpers.CreateConnectionWithCapturingTelemetry returns (connection, capturedLogs) tuple
Helpers use environment variables for connection properties
Solution builds successfully
test_requirements: Infra classes compile and can be used by test methods
CapturingTelemetryExporter correctly stores exported logs
Helpers correctly configure connection with TestExporterFactory

title: Implement comprehensive E2E telemetry tests for the complete pipeline
description: Create comprehensive E2E tests verifying the complete telemetry pipeline from driver operation to captured telemetry logs. Test connection events, statement execution events, error events, CloudFetch metrics, multi-connection client sharing, circuit breaker behavior, and graceful shutdown. All tests use CapturingTelemetryExporter and TelemetryTestHelpers for test isolation.
design_context: Sprint plan Task 9: E2E tests. Section 10 of telemetry-design.md testing strategy. Tests verify: connection event exported on open, statement event with latency, CloudFetch chunk metrics, error events with error_type, multiple connections share client, circuit breaker stops exporting, graceful shutdown flushes all events, feature flag disabled prevents export.
input_requirements: CapturingTelemetryExporter and TelemetryTestHelpers from previous task
All telemetry components integrated into DatabricksConnection
Activity tags set on connection and statement activities
Live Databricks environment via env vars (DATABRICKS_HOST, DATABRICKS_TOKEN, DATABRICKS_HTTP_PATH)
exit_criteria: Telemetry_Connection_ExportsConnectionEvent: open connection, verify connection event captured
Telemetry_Statement_ExportsStatementEvent: execute SELECT 1, verify statement event with latency
Telemetry_Error_ExportsErrorEvent: invalid SQL, verify error event with error.type
Telemetry_FeatureFlagDisabled_NoExport: feature flag off, no events exported
Telemetry_MultipleConnections_SharesClient: 2+ connections to same host, single client
Telemetry_GracefulShutdown_FlushesEvents: close with pending events, all flushed
All E2E tests pass against live Databricks environment
test_requirements: Telemetry_Connection_ExportsConnectionEvent: verify session.id, driver.version in captured log
Telemetry_Statement_ExportsStatementEvent: verify statement.id, operation_latency_ms > 0
Telemetry_CloudFetch_ExportsChunkMetrics: large query, verify chunk_count, bytes_downloaded (if CloudFetch enabled)

Telemetry_MultipleConnections_SharesClient: 3 connections same host, single TelemetryClient
Telemetry_GracefulShutdown_FlushesEvents: execute queries, close, verify all events captured
Tests may use [Trait] or similar for E2E categorization
